object_id.description: Normalized object identifier used as the stable candidate key.

canonical_name.description: Human-readable canonical name chosen from inventory evidence and optional Stage-1 prior.

entity_type.description: Dominant object category for the candidate.

candidate_source.description: Where the candidate came from: inventory_with_stage1_prior / inventory_only / stage1_fallback.

candidate_stats.description: Gap and semantic scores actually used for validation and ranking.

inventory_support.description: Summary of scene-inventory support: scene count, source channels, and direct-visual-evidence flag.

stage1_prior.description: Matched Stage-1 event used as a prior, or null if this is inventory-only.

stage1_supports.description: All distinct Stage-1 prior traces merged into this candidate after deduplication.

possible_chekhov_score.description: Heuristic score in [0,1] estimating whether the object exhibits a setup -> absence -> payoff pattern across scenes.

possible_chekhov_label.description: Discrete bucket derived from possible_chekhov_score: high / medium / low / unlikely.

validation_reasons.description: Human-readable reason tags explaining why the object was kept as a narrative candidate.

setup_scene_indices.description: Earliest scene indices that serve as setup-side support for the candidate.

payoff_scene_indices.description: Later scene indices that serve as payoff-side support for the candidate.

loose_scene_chain.description: Expanded scene chain for the candidate. This includes inventory matches, matched source channels, climax scores, and optional Stage-1 anchor scenes.

loose_scene_chain.heading: EXT. DURSLEY HOUSE - NIGHT 13A
